have h₁ : (G ↾ M ∆ M').EdgeFinite := by
    have h₂ : (M ∆ M' : Set β).Finite := by
      -- Prove that the symmetric difference M ∆ M' is finite
      have h₃ : (M ∪ M' : Set β).Finite := by
        -- Since G is EdgeFinite and M, M' ⊆ E(G), M and M' are finite
        have h₄ : M ⊆ (G.E : Set β) := hM.subset
        have h₅ : M' ⊆ (G.E : Set β) := hM'.subset
        have h₆ : (G.E : Set β).Finite := by
          -- G is EdgeFinite, so G.E is finite
          exact inferInstance
        -- The union of M and M' is a subset of G.E, hence finite
        exact Set.Finite.subset h₆ (by simp_all [Set.subset_union_left, Set.subset_union_right])
      -- M ∆ M' = (M ∪ M') \ (M ∩ M'), and both (M ∪ M') and (M ∩ M') are finite
      have h₇ : (M ∩ M' : Set β).Finite := by
        have h₈ : (M ∩ M' : Set β) ⊆ (M ∪ M' : Set β) := by
          intro x hx
          exact Set.mem_of_mem_inter_right hx
        exact Set.Finite.subset h₃ h₈
      -- Symmetric difference of finite sets is finite
      exact Set.Finite.subset h₃ (fun x hx ↦ by simp_all [Set.mem_union, Set.mem_symmDiff, Set.mem_inter_iff])
    -- G ↾ (M ∆ M') is EdgeFinite because M ∆ M' is finite and G is EdgeFinite
    have h₃ : (G ↾ (M ∆ M')).EdgeFinite := by
      -- Use the fact that G is EdgeFinite and M ∆ M' is finite to deduce EdgeFinite for the restriction
      refine' inferInstance
    exact h₃
  -- Remove loops preserves EdgeFinite
  have h₂ : (G ↾ (M ∆ M') |>.loopRemove).EdgeFinite := by
    -- LoopRemoval does not increase the number of edges, hence preserves EdgeFinite
    infer_instance
  exact h₂
